agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "A logistics platform managing the lifecycle of parcel deliveries, from shipment creation through transit to final delivery. Revenue is driven by weight-based pricing across three brackets. The domain enforces strict state machine transitions to ensure shipment integrity.",
    "bounded_context": "Shipment Management — covers shipment creation, state transitions, weight validation, and weight-bracket classification for pricing.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Shipment", "definition": "A parcel delivery unit created with a pickup address and delivery address, progressing through defined lifecycle states." },
      { "term": "Pickup Address", "definition": "The origin location where the parcel is collected from the sender." },
      { "term": "Delivery Address", "definition": "The destination location where the parcel is delivered to the recipient." },
      { "term": "Shipment State", "definition": "The current lifecycle phase of a shipment: Pending, In Transit, or Delivered." },
      { "term": "Pending", "definition": "Initial state after shipment creation; the parcel has not yet been picked up for transport." },
      { "term": "In Transit", "definition": "The parcel has been picked up and is actively being transported toward the delivery address." },
      { "term": "Delivered", "definition": "Terminal state indicating the parcel has been successfully handed off at the delivery address. Irreversible." },
      { "term": "Weight", "definition": "The physical mass of the parcel in kilograms, must be positive and at most 1000 kg." },
      { "term": "Weight Bracket", "definition": "A pricing classification derived from shipment weight: Standard (0–10 kg], Heavy (10–50 kg], or Freight (>50 kg)." },
      { "term": "Standard", "definition": "Weight bracket for parcels weighing more than 0 kg up to and including 10 kg." },
      { "term": "Heavy", "definition": "Weight bracket for parcels weighing more than 10 kg up to and including 50 kg." },
      { "term": "Freight", "definition": "Weight bracket for parcels weighing more than 50 kg up to and including 1000 kg." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "A shipment can only be created when both a pickup address and a delivery address are provided.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "A shipment without both endpoints is logically incoherent — there is no physical delivery possible. This is a data integrity constraint, not a policy choice.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any attempt to create a shipment with a null/empty pickup address or a null/empty delivery address must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "Shipment weight must be strictly positive (> 0 kg).",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "A parcel with zero or negative weight is physically impossible. This is a physical constraint ensuring data integrity.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any shipment with weight <= 0 must be rejected at creation and at any subsequent weight update."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "Shipment weight must not exceed 1000 kg.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The 1000 kg cap is a business policy reflecting current fleet/infrastructure limits. A VP could raise this limit if the company acquires heavier-capacity vehicles.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any shipment with weight > 1000 kg must be rejected at creation and at any subsequent weight update."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "A shipment in the In Transit state cannot transition back to Pending.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "This encodes the physical reality that once a parcel is picked up and moving, it has left the origin. Allowing regression to Pending would corrupt tracking integrity and violate the real-world process.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any state change request from In Transit to Pending must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "A Delivered shipment is in a terminal state — no further state transitions are permitted.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Delivery is a real-world finality event (handoff occurred). Allowing state changes after delivery would corrupt audit trails, trigger incorrect billing adjustments, and misrepresent physical reality.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any state change request on a shipment in the Delivered state must be rejected, regardless of the target state."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "Weight bracket classification: Standard is weight in (0, 10] kg; Heavy is weight in (10, 50] kg; Freight is weight in (50, 1000] kg.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The bracket boundaries and names are pricing policy. A VP could adjust thresholds (e.g., Standard up to 15 kg) without corrupting system integrity.",
      "testable_assertion": "A 10 kg parcel must classify as Standard; a 10.01 kg parcel must classify as Heavy; a 50 kg parcel must classify as Heavy; a 50.01 kg parcel must classify as Freight."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "The only valid shipment state transitions are: Pending → In Transit, and In Transit → Delivered.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "This is the minimal forward-only state machine derived from the physical delivery process. Skipping states (e.g., Pending → Delivered) would bypass operational steps and corrupt tracking data.",
      "testable_assertion": "A shipment in Pending can only transition to In Transit. A shipment In Transit can only transition to Delivered. No other transitions are permitted."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [
    {
      "id": "AC-1",
      "given": "A valid pickup address and a valid delivery address and a weight of 5 kg",
      "when": "A shipment is created",
      "then": "The shipment is created in Pending state with weight bracket Standard"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-2",
      "given": "A missing or empty pickup address",
      "when": "A shipment creation is attempted",
      "then": "The creation is rejected with a validation error indicating pickup address is required"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-3",
      "given": "A missing or empty delivery address",
      "when": "A shipment creation is attempted",
      "then": "The creation is rejected with a validation error indicating delivery address is required"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-4",
      "given": "A weight of 0 kg or a negative weight",
      "when": "A shipment creation is attempted",
      "then": "The creation is rejected with a validation error indicating weight must be positive"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-5",
      "given": "A weight of 1001 kg",
      "when": "A shipment creation is attempted",
      "then": "The creation is rejected with a validation error indicating weight must not exceed 1000 kg"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-6",
      "given": "A shipment in Pending state",
      "when": "The shipment is transitioned to In Transit",
      "then": "The state changes to In Transit successfully"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-7",
      "given": "A shipment in In Transit state",
      "when": "A transition to Pending is requested",
      "then": "The transition is rejected with an error indicating In Transit cannot revert to Pending"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-8",
      "given": "A shipment in In Transit state",
      "when": "The shipment is transitioned to Delivered",
      "then": "The state changes to Delivered successfully"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-9",
      "given": "A shipment in Delivered state",
      "when": "Any state transition is attempted (to Pending or In Transit)",
      "then": "The transition is rejected with an error indicating Delivered is a terminal state"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-10",
      "given": "A shipment in Pending state",
      "when": "A transition directly to Delivered is requested",
      "then": "The transition is rejected — shipments cannot skip the In Transit state"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-11",
      "given": "A shipment with weight exactly 10 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is calculated",
      "then": "The bracket is Standard"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-12",
      "given": "A shipment with weight 10.01 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is calculated",
      "then": "The bracket is Heavy"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-13",
      "given": "A shipment with weight exactly 50 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is calculated",
      "then": "The bracket is Heavy"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-14",
      "given": "A shipment with weight 50.01 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is calculated",
      "then": "The bracket is Freight"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-15",
      "given": "A shipment with weight exactly 1000 kg",
      "when": "A shipment creation is attempted",
      "then": "The shipment is created successfully with weight bracket Freight"
    }
  ],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Weight at exact boundary of 10 kg", "expected_behavior": "Classified as Standard (bracket is 0–10 inclusive). The PM said '0-10 kg is standard' which includes the boundary." },
    { "scenario": "Weight at exact boundary of 50 kg", "expected_behavior": "Classified as Heavy (bracket is 10–50 inclusive). The PM said '10-50 kg is heavy' which includes the boundary." },
    { "scenario": "Weight of exactly 1000 kg", "expected_behavior": "Accepted as valid; classified as Freight. 1000 kg is the maximum, not above it." },
    { "scenario": "Weight with high decimal precision (e.g., 9.999999 kg)", "expected_behavior": "Should classify as Standard. Implementation must handle floating-point comparisons correctly at bracket boundaries." },
    { "scenario": "Attempting Pending → Delivered (skipping In Transit)", "expected_behavior": "Rejected. The state machine is strictly linear: Pending → In Transit → Delivered. No state may be skipped." },
    { "scenario": "Attempting In Transit → In Transit (self-transition)", "expected_behavior": "Spec is silent. Recommend rejecting as a no-op to keep the state machine strict, but this needs clarification." },
    { "scenario": "Shipment created with very small positive weight (e.g., 0.001 kg)", "expected_behavior": "Accepted as valid; classified as Standard. Weight must only be positive — no minimum threshold beyond zero is stated." },
    { "scenario": "Addresses that are syntactically present but semantically invalid (e.g., empty string, whitespace only)", "expected_behavior": "Should be rejected. 'Provided' means meaningfully present, not just non-null." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Weight bracket classification directly drives revenue. Misclassification at boundaries (especially 10 kg and 50 kg) means under- or over-charging customers. Getting the boundary conditions wrong at scale could create systemic revenue leakage or customer disputes.",
    "operational": "The forward-only state machine ensures driver dispatch, warehouse operations, and tracking systems can trust shipment state. Allowing state regression would break downstream operational workflows (e.g., re-dispatching a delivered parcel).",
    "user": "Customers and recipients rely on accurate state tracking for visibility. Terminal Delivered state gives confidence that handoff occurred. Weight bracket transparency at creation time sets pricing expectations.",
    "risk": "Primary risks are (1) boundary misclassification causing billing disputes, (2) state machine violations corrupting audit trails and operational trust, (3) missing address validation allowing undeliverable shipments to enter the system.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Shippers (senders)", "impact": "Pricing accuracy at creation time; trust that shipments progress correctly through the lifecycle." },
      { "group": "Recipients", "impact": "Delivery tracking accuracy; confidence in Delivered status finality." },
      { "group": "Operations / Dispatch", "impact": "State integrity ensures correct driver assignments and warehouse workflows. Regression would cause double-handling." },
      { "group": "Finance / Billing", "impact": "Weight bracket accuracy directly affects invoicing correctness and revenue recognition." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Clear state machine reduces ambiguity in dispute resolution. Terminal states simplify 'where is my parcel?' inquiries." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 72,
    "reasoning": "The PM's spec is clear on state transitions and weight constraints, but has ambiguities around bracket boundary inclusivity (does '0-10' include 10 or not?), address validation depth, whether weight can change after creation, and whether self-transitions are allowed. These are common spec gaps that could lead to implementation divergence.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Forward-only state machine: Pending → In Transit → Delivered",
      "Delivered is terminal — no transitions out",
      "In Transit cannot revert to Pending",
      "Weight must be positive and ≤ 1000 kg",
      "Three weight brackets exist: Standard, Heavy, Freight",
      "Shipment requires both pickup and delivery addresses"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Exact bracket boundary inclusivity (is 10 kg Standard or Heavy?)",
      "Whether weight can be modified after shipment creation",
      "Whether Pending → Delivered (skipping In Transit) is explicitly forbidden or just unstated",
      "Self-transitions (e.g., In Transit → In Transit) — accept or reject?",
      "Address validation depth — what constitutes a 'valid' address?",
      "Whether shipment cancellation exists as a concept (spec is silent)"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "Bracket boundaries follow the natural reading: '0-10 kg' means up to and including 10, '10-50 kg' means above 10 up to and including 50, 'over 50 kg' means above 50",
      "Pending → Delivered is forbidden because the spec describes a linear lifecycle and only mentions forward transitions",
      "New shipments always start in Pending state",
      "Weight is set at creation and the spec does not describe weight modification after creation",
      "There is no cancellation state — the spec does not mention one"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The PM says '0-10 kg is standard, 10-50 kg is heavy' — is exactly 10 kg Standard or Heavy? The boundary is claimed by both brackets as written. We assumed inclusive-lower, inclusive-upper for each named range (so 10 kg = Standard, 50 kg = Heavy), but this needs PM confirmation.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Finance / Billing", "Shippers (senders)"],
      "options": ["10 kg is Standard (upper-inclusive for lower bracket)", "10 kg is Heavy (lower-inclusive for upper bracket)"],
      "recommendation": "10 kg is Standard, 50 kg is Heavy — matching the natural reading of '0-10' and '10-50' where each range owns its upper bound. Confirm with PM."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "The spec does not mention shipment cancellation. In real logistics, shipments in Pending or even In Transit may need to be cancelled. Is there a Cancelled state?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Shippers (senders)", "Operations / Dispatch", "Customer Support"],
      "options": ["Add a Cancelled state reachable from Pending and In Transit", "No cancellation — out of scope for now", "Cancellation only from Pending"],
      "recommendation": "Clarify with PM. Most logistics platforms support cancellation at least from Pending. This is a significant domain gap."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "Can shipment weight be modified after creation? Real-world weighing often happens at pickup, not at booking. If weight changes, the bracket and pricing must be recalculated.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Finance / Billing", "Shippers (senders)"],
      "options": ["Weight is immutable after creation", "Weight can be updated only while in Pending state", "Weight can be updated until Delivered"],
      "recommendation": "Allow weight update only while Pending (before physical pickup). Confirm with PM."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": []
}
